image0.name: step_6_plot_0_1778590772.png
image0.caption: Ensemble-averaged matter power spectrum from 10 simulations using 2LPT initial conditions. Top: The mean simulated power spectrum, $\langle P\_{sim}(k) \rangle$, is compared to the non-linear prediction from CAMB HaloFit, $P\_{CAMB\_NL}(k)$. Bottom: The ratio of the simulated power to the theoretical prediction. The simulation agrees with the theoretical model to within 5\% at large scales ($k < 0.03$ h/Mpc), while systematically underpredicting power at smaller scales ($k > 0.1$ h/Mpc).

image1.name: pk_ensemble.png
image1.caption: The ensemble-averaged matter power spectrum from 10 Warp PM simulations using 2LPT initial conditions (red line) compared against the CAMB HaloFit theoretical prediction (black line). The left panel shows the absolute power spectra, while the right panel shows their ratio. The shaded region represents the 1σ cosmic variance across the ensemble, which is significant at large scales (k < 0.05 h/Mpc) but becomes small at higher k. The simulation achieves agreement within 5\% for k < 0.03 h/Mpc, but systematically underpredicts power at smaller scales, with a growing deficit for k > 0.1 h/Mpc.

image2.name: pk_500steps.png
image2.caption: The matter power spectrum from a 10-realization ensemble of Particle-Mesh simulations, initialized with second-order Lagrangian Perturbation Theory (2LPT) and evolved over 500 steps, compared against the theoretical CAMB HaloFit prediction. The left panel shows the absolute power spectra, with the ensemble mean (red dashed line) and 1σ scatter (shaded area). The right panel displays the ratio of the simulated to the theoretical power spectrum, demonstrating that the simulation achieves the target 5\% accuracy (indicated by the grey dotted lines) over the wavenumber range k = 0.04–0.22 h/Mpc. Deviations at low k are dominated by cosmic variance, while the power suppression at high k is due to the simulation's force resolution limit.
